ci-organizer-create-folders.title: Create organizer folders
ci-organizer-create-folders.prompt:
Create `school/notes`, `school/homework`, `school/code`, and `backups`.

Use the terminal. The editor is hidden on purpose for this task.
ci-organizer-create-folders.hint: Use terminal commands such as `pwd`, `ls`, `mkdir -p`, `touch`, `cp`, `mv`, or `rm` depending on the task.

help.concept: This is a Linux terminal workspace task. Complete it by changing the file tree with terminal commands.
help.hint_1: Use `pwd` and `ls` to inspect where you are.
help.hint_2: The checker looks for the required folders/files and checks that forbidden files are gone.

ci-organizer-create-folders.placeholder: Use the terminal to complete this task.

ci-organizer-move-files.title: Move files into the right folders
ci-organizer-move-files.prompt:
Move `notes.txt`, `homework.txt`, and `project.sh` from `messy-inbox` into their correct school folders.

Use the terminal. The editor is hidden on purpose for this task.
ci-organizer-move-files.hint: Use terminal commands such as `pwd`, `ls`, `mkdir -p`, `touch`, `cp`, `mv`, or `rm` depending on the task.

help.concept: This is a Linux terminal workspace task. Complete it by changing the file tree with terminal commands.
help.hint_1: Use `pwd` and `ls` to inspect where you are.
help.hint_2: The checker looks for the required folders/files and checks that forbidden files are gone.

ci-organizer-move-files.placeholder: Use the terminal to complete this task.

ci-organizer-final.title: Finish the organizer
ci-organizer-final.prompt:
Copy `school/notes/notes.txt` to `backups/notes-backup.txt` and remove `messy-inbox/old.tmp`.

Use the terminal. The editor is hidden on purpose for this task.
ci-organizer-final.hint: Use terminal commands such as `pwd`, `ls`, `mkdir -p`, `touch`, `cp`, `mv`, or `rm` depending on the task.

help.concept: This is a Linux terminal workspace task. Complete it by changing the file tree with terminal commands.
help.hint_1: Use `pwd` and `ls` to inspect where you are.

ci-organizer-final.placeholder: Use the terminal to complete this task.

organizer-goal.title: Turn a messy folder into a clean workspace
organizer-goal.bodyMarkdown:
Real developer folders get messy. In this project, you will organize loose files into purpose-based folders.

You will use:

```bash
mkdir
cp
mv
rm
ls
```

The checker will confirm the final file tree.

organizer-safe-plan.title: Plan before moving files
organizer-safe-plan.bodyMarkdown:
Before moving files, inspect the starting folder:

```bash
ls messy-inbox
```

Then create destination folders before moving anything:

```bash
mkdir -p school/notes school/homework school/code backups
```

organizer-final-check.title: A clean final tree tells a story
organizer-final-check.bodyMarkdown: At the end, your workspace should make sense to another person. Notes go in `school/notes`, homework in `school/homework`, code in `school/code`, and important copies in `backups`.
